raw_excerpt: Hermes Agent Masterclass by CyrilXBT (@cyrilXBT), May 30 2026. Long-form X thread / masterclass covering Sections 1-7+: What Hermes Agent Actually Is (open source autonomous agent framework by Nous Research, MIT license, Feb 2026; four properties — persistent memory, skill system, scheduled automation, MCP integration); Installation (Node 18+, .env config, MODEL_PROVIDER/ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/MEMORY_BACKEND/SCHEDULER_TIMEZONE/LOG_PATH); File Architecture (~/.hermes/ tree: config.yaml, .env, SOUL.md, memories/MEMORY.md+USER.md, skills/, sessions/, state.db with FTS5, cron/jobs.json, plugins/, hooks/, logs/); Skills (SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter — name, description, version, author, platforms; skill_manage tool with create/patch/edit/delete actions; progressive disclosure for token cost; 30-day stale, 90-day archived); Memory (state.db SQLite with FTS5, Honcho user modeling); Profiles (each profile = isolated Hermes instance with own config/memory/skills/sessions/SOUL.md, share nothing by default); Multi-platform gateways (Telegram/Discord/Slack/15+); Cron (cronjob tool, can chain via context_from); Self-evolution repo (NousResearch/hermes-agent-self-evolution, offline optimization pipeline, GEPA scoring, constraint gates 100% test pass + skills <15KB); 90-turn cap per task; SOUL.md is slot #1 in system prompt; learning loop = agent encounters problem → solves → saves as SKILL.md → next time loads it. Documented onboarding gap: 'Most people who discover Hermes Agent spend the first week confused.' Republished to dailydoseofds.com as long-form article.
trust_notes: Single-source. Social (X post by @cyrilXBT). Republished to dailydoseofds.com as long-form article. CyrilXBT is a third-party X user (handle context, not Hermes project staff). Notre Research's own docs (hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/) cover the same content. Trust: 0.5 secondary, 0.6 if treated as a long-form newsletter rather than a tweet.
